subject: Mathematics
title: Sofia Kovalevskaya: Breaking Barriers
description: A 60-second lesson on Sofia Kovalevskaya: Breaking Barriers.

hook.text: No European university would admit women. So Sofia Kovalevskaya entered a fake marriage to leave Russia.

buildup.text: She studied privately with top mathematicians in Germany and earned a doctorate in 1874.

discovery.text: She solved the rotation of rigid bodies—a problem that had stumped mathematicians for decades.

twist.text: Even with a doctorate, no university would hire her. She spent years unable to find work.

climax.text: She finally became the first female math professor in Northern Europe, at Stockholm University.

punchline.text: She faked a marriage to study and became a professor.

quiz.question: Why did Kovalevskaya enter a marriage of convenience?
options: To leave Russia and attend university abroad
